coupling_discount_applied._note: Adversary flagged 3 coupled untested mechanisms targeting Skeptical Investor (cta_primary_label, refund_or_guarantee_copy, trade_evidence — all share 'show-a-real-thing' honesty substrate). Synthesize used 0.7 non-linearity discount assuming independent mechanisms; estimator applies 0.5 per adversary's analysis. The ratio 0.5/0.7 = 0.714 multiplies the Skeptical lift component.

skeptical_investor.revision_reason: Baseline Wilson widens low to 0.09 (n=12 on a 25% rate; at 95% CI, V4's true Skeptical rate could have been anywhere from 9% to 53%). Coupled-mechanism discount (0.5 vs 0.7) compresses the lift component from [+5,+15] to [+4,+11]pt. Estimator low becomes Wilson baseline low (mechanism could fail entirely). Estimator high: V4 + revised lift_high.
kill_conditions: real_closed_trade backend delivers a loss trade during peak traffic window (first-session failure rate > 10% of sessions)
refund SLA p90 exceeds 60s in first 500 trials (promised-but-undelivered → trust violation larger than V4 baseline)
'See 1 real trade, free' flow requires ₹1 before showing the trade (mismatch relocates to post-click, drop-off > 40% at that step)
Skeptical Investor conversion ≤ 27% after 2 weeks of post-ship data (2pt above V4 = mechanisms not transferring)

curious_beginner.revision_reason: Baseline Wilson widens low to 0.15. No coupling concerns for this segment; mechanism lift from synthesize preserved.
kill_conditions: Stocks in named-wins carousel don't resonate with real user cohort (engagement time on carousel < 2s avg)
real_closed_trade visually displaces the named-wins carousel and the Curious anchor mechanism doesn't land
Curious Beginner conversion within ±2pt of V4 (33%) indicates the +7pt mechanism lift failed

bargain_hunter.revision_reason: Baseline Wilson widens dramatically — at n=13, a 69% rate has 95% CI [42%, 87%]. Bargain Hunter is near ceiling; mechanism lift is small (0-4pt). Point estimate preserved.
kill_conditions: Added trust/evidence elements slow the 7-second decision flow to >10s (cognitive load negates the conversion speed advantage)
Dual-stack removal removes a psychological 'two options' hook that was part of V4's Bargain draw (Bargain < 65%)

trust_seeker.revision_reason: Baseline Wilson at n=10 is especially wide [24%, 76%]. This is the segment with the smallest sample — every conclusion about Trust Seekers is correspondingly less reliable. No coupling discount (Trust mechanisms differ from Skeptical's). Mechanism lift preserved.
kill_conditions: V5 Trust Seeker ≥ 13pt below V1's 60% (i.e., Trust Seeker < 47%) indicates the green CTA penalty is larger than the V2→V3 contrast measured, and muted_premium should have been the primary cta_style choice
Restored trust_signal + evidence_detail do not offset green penalty (Trust < 48%)
Fatigue effect: Trust Seekers dismiss SEBI + named wins as pattern-matched compliance (Trust at V4 parity, 50%)

lift_over_baseline_pts.note: Low tier is negative — V5 could underperform V4 within the honest Wilson confidence band. This is not a prediction that V5 WILL fail; it reflects the small-sample uncertainty on the V4 baseline itself. Per-segment Wilson bands compound.

weighted_overall_estimate.revision_note: Wilson intervals on baselines widen the low tier dramatically (n=10-15 per segment means a 25% rate has 95% CI ranging [9%, 53%]). Synthesize's [45.5%, 53.0%] interval was too narrow — it reflected only mechanism uncertainty, not baseline uncertainty. Estimator combines both. Point estimate barely moves (49.3% → 48.6%); the honest picture is a wide band with the central tendency intact.

revised_recommendation.ship_decision_framework: V5 is defensible at the point estimate (48.6% weighted overall, +4.6pt over V4). The wide low tier is a correct reflection of the 5-variant × n=10-15 dataset's information content, not evidence that V5 will fail. The ship decision rests on whether Univest accepts the Operational Preconditions (adversary blockers 1-3). Without them, the low tier risk is real. With them, the band tightens toward the point.
pre_ship_actions: Resolve adversary blocker 1 (legal + flow on 'free' language)
Resolve adversary blocker 2 (refund SLA ops signoff)
Consider A/B: V5a (green) vs V5b (muted_premium) per adversary obj-005

flags: Wilson intervals widen low tier to -22pt vs V4 — NOT a prediction V5 fails. It's the honest uncertainty on the baseline at n=10-15 per segment. Synthesize's previous [45.5%, 53.0%] was an artifact of only counting mechanism uncertainty.

Coupled-mechanism discount applied only to Skeptical (3 untested mechanisms share honesty substrate per adversary). Curious/Bargain/Trust retain synthesize's assumption of independent mechanisms — they have 0-1 untested mechanisms each.

To narrow the V5 interval, the binding constraint is sample size in future simulations, not algorithm refinement. The next Univest simulation should use n ≥ 30 per segment minimum to narrow Wilson bands to usable widths.
